skill.name: neurokit2
skill.description: Reference guide for NeuroKit2 biosignal processing workflows, including ECG, EEG, EDA, RSP, PPG, EMG, EOG, HRV, epochs, complexity, and general signal utilities.
skill.summary: Analyze physiological signals with NeuroKit2 workflows and reference guidance.
skill.author: davila7

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many external command and high-risk blocker patterns, but review found they are documentation false positives in Markdown examples and scientific terminology. No prompt injection, hidden execution path, data exfiltration, or malicious intent was found. Residual risk is low because the community skill includes install examples and public external links.

low_findings.title: Markdown Examples Misclassified as External Commands
low_findings.description: The external command findings are false positives. The cited lines are Markdown code fences or inline Python examples, not skill code that executes shell commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed locations contain fenced Markdown examples such as Python snippets and installation text. No executable script, hook, or runtime wrapper invokes these commands.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URLs Are Public Documentation Links
low_findings.description: The network findings are low risk. They point to NeuroKit documentation, the upstream GitHub repository, a DOI, and a development installation example rather than hidden exfiltration endpoints.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are visible user-facing references to the official project and publication. The only command is an explicit install example that a user would choose to run.

low_findings.title: Scientific Terms Misclassified as Malware Indicators
low_findings.description: The high blocker findings are false positives from domain language. Terms such as HRV acceleration and deceleration, ECG method names, and signal-processing labels are scientific documentation, not C2, reconnaissance, or cryptographic behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The surrounding context is physiological signal analysis documentation. I found no code that performs cryptography, network reconnaissance, system reconnaissance, or command-and-control behavior.

content.value_statement: Physiological signal analysis requires many specialized functions and parameter choices. This skill provides NeuroKit2 workflows for common biosignal processing tasks.
actual_capabilities: Guides ECG and PPG processing, peak detection, quality assessment, and HRV analysis.
Documents EEG power, microstate, channel quality, and source localization workflows.
Covers EDA, RSP, EMG, and EOG processing with signal-specific NeuroKit2 functions.
Explains filtering, resampling, peak correction, decomposition, and complexity measures.
Supports event-related epochs and multi-signal integration with NeuroKit2 bio workflows.
limitations: It provides reference guidance and examples, but it does not execute NeuroKit2 itself.
Users must install Python dependencies and provide valid physiological signal data.
Clinical interpretation requires domain expertise and appropriate validation.
Large recordings may need separate performance planning and data management.

use_cases.title: Process Lab ECG Data
use_cases.description: Select NeuroKit2 functions for ECG cleaning, R-peak detection, quality checks, and HRV feature extraction.

use_cases.title: Compare Multimodal Signals
use_cases.description: Combine ECG, EDA, respiration, and EMG workflows to study arousal, load, and task responses.

use_cases.title: Build Reproducible Pipelines
use_cases.description: Use documented processing steps, parameter notes, and output expectations to structure biosignal notebooks.

prompt_templates.title: Choose a Workflow
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner selection of the right signal module
prompt_templates.prompt: I have [signal type] data sampled at [rate] Hz. Recommend a NeuroKit2 workflow and explain the main outputs.

prompt_templates.title: Plan HRV Analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me analyze HRV from ECG data. Include preprocessing, R-peak detection, artifact correction, and time or frequency metrics.

prompt_templates.title: Design Event Analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: Design an event-related analysis for [signals] around [event type]. Include epochs, baseline correction, and summary features.

prompt_templates.title: Audit a Pipeline
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced review of an existing analysis plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this NeuroKit2 biosignal pipeline for methodological risks, missing quality checks, and better parameter choices: [pipeline description].

output_examples.input: A user asks how to process a 10-minute ECG recording for HRV.
output_examples.output: The assistant recommends ECG cleaning, R-peak detection, artifact correction, and HRV metrics split into time, frequency, and nonlinear domains.

output_examples.input: A user has ECG, EDA, and respiration signals from a stress task.
output_examples.output: The assistant proposes synchronized preprocessing, event epochs, EDA response extraction, respiratory rate measures, and integrated interpretation limits.

output_examples.input: A user wants EEG feature ideas for cognitive workload analysis.
output_examples.output: The assistant suggests frequency band power, channel quality checks, microstate analysis, and cautions about artifact control.

best_practices: Confirm sampling rate, units, and sensor placement before choosing processing parameters.
Inspect signal quality and detected peaks before trusting derived physiological metrics.
Document preprocessing choices, artifact handling, and recording duration for reproducibility.
anti_patterns: Do not interpret HRV, EEG, or EDA metrics as clinical conclusions without validation.
Do not mix signals with different time bases without synchronization checks.
Do not report automated features without visual inspection or quality assessment.

faq.question: What signals does this skill cover?
faq.answer: It covers ECG, PPG, EEG, EDA, RSP, EMG, EOG, HRV, epochs, complexity, and general signal processing.

faq.question: Does the skill run NeuroKit2 code?
faq.answer: No. It provides guidance and examples. The user must run Python code in their own environment.

faq.question: Can it help with heart rate variability?
faq.answer: Yes. It explains HRV time, frequency, nonlinear, and RSA-related workflows using NeuroKit2 functions.

faq.question: Can it analyze clinical data?
faq.answer: It can guide processing steps, but clinical interpretation requires qualified review and validated protocols.

faq.question: Does it support multimodal recordings?
faq.answer: Yes. It documents NeuroKit2 bio workflows for processing multiple physiological signals together.

faq.question: What should users prepare before using it?
faq.answer: Users should know signal type, sampling rate, recording duration, event markers, and expected analysis goals.
